title: quercus turbinella subsp. ajoensis
intro: Quercus turbinella subsp. ajoensis, also known as Ajo Buckeye, is a subspecies of oak tree native to the southwestern United States, particularly in Arizona and New Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Ajo Buckeye include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting immune function. Ajo Buckeye can be prepared into various herbal products, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and other health issues.
